quotes.text: The ball knows. I don't know how to explain it better than that. You hit it and there's this half-second before you look up where your body already knows.
quotes.speaker: Ken Griffey Jr.
quotes.context: Hall of Fame weekend interview discussing his hitting instincts, Cooperstown 2023
quotes.fake_note: Griffey often speaks with a relaxed, almost poetic looseness about his natural gifts, and this matches his known reluctance to over-analyze his mechanics. The subtle giveaway is the phrasing 'the ball knows' — Griffey tends to attribute the feel to his hands or his swing, not the ball itself.

quotes.text: People talk about winning like it's the finish line. For me, winning is just the moment when the work you've already done finally gets to show itself.

quotes.text: My dad used to say the ocean doesn't apologize for the wave. I think about that every time someone tells me I'm too aggressive out there.
quotes.speaker: Megan Rapinoe
